History of Present Illness:
44 yo male presents with multiple stab wounds over left chest
and flank with diaphragmatic injury and liver laceration.  Pt
was hypotensive and in respiratory distress and had chest tube
placed in ED.

Brief Hospital Course:
Pt went to OR from ED.  Had explorative laporatomy which
revealed diaphragmatic laceration and liver injury.  Pt was then
transfered to ICU, extubated and had his flank wounds closed
with staples.  He was then transferred to the floor and had his
chest tube removed after the output was less than 100cc's over a
shift.  Follow-up chest x-rays revealed no new pneumothorax.

Medications on Admission:
Morphine
Percocet

Discharge Medications:
same as above

Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Multiple stab wounds
Liver laceration
Diaphragmatic perforation
Hemothorax


Discharge Condition:
Good


Discharge Instructions:
Rest, drink plenty of fluids, take medications as prescribed.

Return to emergency department or visit your primary care
provider for any of the following symptoms: fever, chills,
nausea, vomiting, inability to tolerate food or drink, chest
pain, shortness of breath, inability to have a bowel movement,
redness, pus drainage or pain at the site of your surgical
incision or repaired lacerations

Followup Instructions:
Follow up with your primary care provider.

Return to trauma surgery clinic for reevaluation and removal of
sutures on Tuesday 06-10.  Please call 799-795-6457 to make
an appointment.


                             [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] Harris MD, 96184
